# W18T3 Implementation Plan

## Task Goal

Finalize `unique()` special value handling so floating point and complex inputs follow the exact Xenon semantics for `NaN`, signed zero, and component wise complex equality.

## Related Design Documents

- `design/14-set.md` §1.2
- `design/14-set.md` §5.1
- `design/14-set.md` §6.2-§6.5
- `design/14-set.md` §8.2-§8.4
- `design/01-architecture.md` §1.3, §1.5
- `design/00-coding.md` §2.3, §2.5

## Dependencies

- W18T2

## Files to Create/Modify

- `src/set/unique.rs`

## Implementation Details

### Complex support added in this task

Add sealed and `UniqueElement` impls for:

- `Complex<f32>`
- `Complex<f64>`

Use the exact component wise equality bodies from the design:

```rust
impl UniqueElement for Complex<f32> {
    fn unique_eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {
        self.re == other.re && self.im == other.im
    }
}

impl UniqueElement for Complex<f64> {
    fn unique_eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {
        self.re == other.re && self.im == other.im
    }
}
```

### Semantic rules that the implementation must enforce

- `NaN != NaN`, each `NaN` must be preserved as its own output element.
- `-0.0 == 0.0`, signed zero values must collapse into one unique value.
- Complex equality is component wise.
- Each complex component reuses the same floating point rules above.
- Complex numbers with `NaN` in either component must not be merged only because both contain `NaN`.

### Algorithm constraints

- Do not rely directly on standard Rust `Hash` or `Eq` semantics for `f32`, `f64`, `Complex<f32>`, or `Complex<f64>`.
- Do not use `HashSet` or `BTreeSet` as the semantic authority for float or complex deduplication.
- Allowed strategies are:
  - linear scan using `unique_eq`, or
  - custom hash key logic that bypasses all `NaN` values and normalizes `+0.0` and `-0.0` to the same key
- If a hash path is used, complex keys must be `(re_key, im_key)` with the same `NaN` bypass rule for either component.

### Exclusions

- `bool` still must not implement `UniqueElement`.
- Do not add sorting, counts, or set algebra APIs.

## Test Plan

- `test_unique_nan_preserved_f32`
- `test_unique_nan_preserved_f64`
- `test_unique_signed_zero_equal_f32`
- `test_unique_signed_zero_equal_f64`
- `test_unique_basic_complex`
- `test_unique_complex_componentwise`
- `test_unique_high_rank_ixdyn`
- `test_unique_extreme_i64_values`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `Complex<f32>` and `Complex<f64>` implement `UniqueElement`
- [ ] Every input `NaN` is preserved in the output
- [ ] `-0.0` and `0.0` are treated as one unique value
- [ ] Complex equality is component wise
- [ ] Complex `NaN` components are preserved according to the floating point rules
- [ ] No standard set type is used as the semantic source for float or complex deduplication
- [ ] `bool` remains unsupported at compile time

## Development Workflow

1. Add tests for `NaN`, signed zero, and complex cases first.
2. Add complex sealed impls and `UniqueElement` impls.
3. Audit the baseline dedup path so it follows the special value rules exactly.
4. Verify that the algorithm does not accidentally merge `NaN` values.
5. Re run integer and real tests to confirm no regression.
